title: Process Signaling with kill() and raise()
description: A flowchart illustrating how the kill() function sends signals to other processes and how the raise() function sends a signal to the current process itself.
relationships: Process A calls kill() with PID and signal
Kernel validates request and delivers signal to Process B
Process B's execution is interrupted, signal handler runs
A process calls raise(), which is a self-targeting kill()
Signal is delivered to the current process's own handler
